description: SIGHUP signal handler for daemon config reload without restart

userStories.title: SIGHUP handler registration in daemon
userStories.description: As a daemon operator, I want the daemon to register a SIGHUP handler on startup so the signal is caught rather than causing default process termination.
acceptanceCriteria: daemon (start_daemon and run_foreground) registers SIGHUP handler before entering poll loop
handler sets an atomic flag (AtomicBool) that the poll loop checks
existing SIGTERM behavior unchanged
no unsafe code required (nix signal_hook or safe signal abstraction)

userStories.title: Config reload function
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a config::reload_config() function that re-reads config.toml from disk, validates it, and returns Result<Config> so failures are surfaced without crashing the daemon.
acceptanceCriteria: reload_config() re-reads from XDG config path (same as load_config)
parse error returns Err, daemon logs warning and continues with old config
validation error (e.g. poll_interval_secs < 10) returns Err, daemon continues with old config
successful parse + validate returns Ok(Config) with expanded paths

userStories.title: Atomic config swap in poll loop
userStories.description: As a daemon operator, I want the poll loop to atomically swap to the new Config when SIGHUP is received so the change takes effect on the next loop iteration without interrupting an in-progress poll.
acceptanceCriteria: run_poll_loop checks reload flag at top of each loop iteration (after watcher recv / sleep)
on flag set: call reload_config(), on Ok swap local config binding, on Err keep old
reload resets watcher with new watch_dirs if watch_dirs changed
mid-poll safety: reload only happens between poll cycles, never inside git_ops::poll_repo
log::info emitted on successful swap; log::warn on failure with reason

userStories.title: Structured logging on reload
userStories.description: As a daemon operator, I want clear log output when a reload is triggered so I can diagnose config changes from blackbox.log.
acceptanceCriteria: log::info!("SIGHUP received, reloading config") on signal receipt
log::info!("Config reloaded successfully") with changed field summary (watch_dirs, poll_interval_secs) on success
log::warn!("Config reload failed: {err}, keeping previous config") on parse/validate error
log lines are visible in ~/.local/share/blackbox/blackbox.log

userStories.title: `blackbox reload` CLI convenience command
userStories.description: As a user, I want `blackbox reload` to send SIGHUP to the running daemon so I don't need to look up the PID manually.
acceptanceCriteria: new Reload variant added to Commands enum in cli.rs
command reads PID from pid file via daemon::is_daemon_running()
if daemon running: sends SIGHUP via nix::sys::signal::kill, prints 'Reloading config (PID N)'
if daemon not running: prints 'Daemon not running' and exits 0
command is NOT exempt from config check (requires config to exist)
command appears in --help output

userStories.title: Unit tests — config reload logic
userStories.description: As a developer, I want unit tests for reload_config() covering success, parse failure, and validation failure so regressions are caught.
acceptanceCriteria: test: valid config file reloads and returns Ok(Config)
test: TOML parse error returns Err
test: config with poll_interval_secs=5 returns Err (validate fails)
test: reload expands ~ paths (watch_dirs contains tilde path post-reload)
tests use tempfile::tempdir() to write config files, not real XDG path

userStories.title: Integration test — `blackbox reload` CLI
userStories.description: As a developer, I want an integration test verifying `blackbox reload` output when daemon is not running so the happy-path CLI is covered without spawning a real daemon.
acceptanceCriteria: assert_cmd test: `blackbox reload` with no daemon running prints 'Daemon not running'
test sets XDG_DATA_HOME to tempdir to avoid stale PID files from real environment
test uses existing assert_cmd + predicates pattern from tests/ directory

userStories.title: Update README.md and AGENTS.md
userStories.description: Update project documentation to reflect new feature. README.md: add new command to Commands table if applicable, update feature descriptions. AGENTS.md: add new source files to Architecture tree, update Key Patterns if new patterns introduced.
acceptanceCriteria: README.md Commands table includes new command (if a CLI command was added)
README.md describes the new feature/capability
AGENTS.md Architecture src/ tree lists any new .rs files
AGENTS.md Key Dependencies updated if new crates added
userStories.notes: This must be the LAST story executed. Reference README.md and AGENTS.md in project root.
